REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. THE COURT HAS PREVIOUSLY RULED ON THE DEMURER TO THE ORIGINAL PETITION. THE RULING STANDS. SPECIFICALLY, THE DEMURRER TO PETITIONER'S CLAIMS ARISING OUT OF SB-35 HAS BEEN AND REMAINS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PETITIONER CHOSE NOT TO PROCEED WITH THE ALLOWED AMENDMENT ("TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO STATE A CLAIM REGARDING THE PURPORTED VIOLATION OF HAA AND SPECIFY AN APPLICATION OTHER THAN SB-35 APPLICATION") IN THE NEXT TWO ITERATIONS OF THE COMPLAINT SHOWING THAT NO SUCH AMENDMENT IS POSSIBLE. THE DEMURRER TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF HAS BEEN AND REMAINS OVERRULED. THEREFORE, AT THIS TIME THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND FOR REASONS STATED HEREIN AND IN THE ORDER ON THE DEMURRER TO THE ORIGINAL PETITION; AND THE DEMURRER TO THE DECLARATORY RELIEF IS OVERRULED. RESPONDENT TO FILE AN ANSWER WITHIN 10 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: VANESSA WU; REPORTED. PEGGY TSUIJIMOTO, CSR 5229, PEGGYTSUJIMOTO@GMAIL.COM, 415-828-1919. (D501)

calendar.matters:
DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Feb-24-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: In-person Appearances

Brian O'Neil Paterson & O'Neil PC, 415-907-9100, for petitioner
Kristen Jensen, Deputy City Atterney, 415-554-4615, for Respondent

Mr. O'Neil contests to the tentative. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. The Court has previously ruled on the Demurer to the original petition. The ruling stands. Specifically, the demurrer to petitioner's claims arising out of SB-35 has been and remains sustained without leave to amend. Petitioner chose not to proceed with the allowed amendment ("to amend for Plaintiff to state a claim regarding the purported violation of HAA and specify an application other than SB-35 application") in the next two iterations of the Complaint showing that no such amendment is possible. The demurrer to the cause of action for declaratory relief has been and remains OVERRULED. Therefore, at this time the demurrer to the first cause of action is sustained without leave to amend for reasons stated herein and in the order on the demurrer to the original petition; and the demurrer to the declaratory relief is OVERRULED. Respondent to file an answer within 10 days of notice of entry of order.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Reported. Peggy Tsuijimoto, CSR 5229, peggytsujimoto@gmail.com, 415-828-1919. (D501)

DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Aug-01-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION

Appearances: Kristen Jensen of the Office of the City Attorney (415.554.4700) for respondent and moving party; Brian O'Neill and Ryan Patterson of Patterson & O'Neill (415.907.9110) for petitioners and responding parties.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part.

Demurrer is sustained to the first cause of action for a writ of mandate with leave to amend for Plaintiff to state a claim regarding the purported violation of HAA and specify an application other than SB-35 application. Demurrer to the first cause of action for writ of mandate is sustained without leave to amend to the extent it is based on the purported misinterpretation within HCD guidelines followed by the City. Petitioner failed to cite any authority for the proposition that the City may disregard these guidelines in favor of Plaintiff's interpretation of SB-35.

Demurrer to the cause of action for declaratory relief is overruled. While the cause of action for writ of mandate and declaratory relief may overlap, the cause of action for declaratory relief is not entirely derivative, e.g. the alleged policy detailed in Planning Director Bulletin 5 and SB-35 application form. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Jane Hong, CSR 11975 (T: 909.831.2644, E: csrjane@hotmail.com) (501/CFH)
